data.title: Labat Africa Appoints N. Bodirwa As Interim Chairperson
data.description:
Labat Africa Ltd :

data.title: Australian police officer who fatally Tasered 95-year-old grandmother avoids jail
data.description:
An Australian police officer who was convicted of manslaughter for fatally shooting a 95-year-old care home resident with a Taser gun avoided jail time on Friday.

Senior constable Kristian White, who was fired after being convicted for the death of Clare Nowland in 2023, will instead serve a two-year community correction order - effectively a good behaviour bond - and 425 hours of community service.

Judge Ian Harrison said White's offence fell "at the lower end of objective seriousness" for manslaughter.

Members of Nowland’s family, who filled multiple rows of the public gallery to hear the decision, burst into tears as the sentence was handed down, local media reported.

"It’s a slap on the wrist for someone that’s killed our mother … I need time to process that," Nowland’s oldest son, Michael, said outside the Supreme Court of New South Wales in Sydney.

"Justice and fairness - that’s all we wanted."

White’s lawyer Warwick Anderson said his client and his family were “very relieved” with the outcome.

White, 35, was found guilty of manslaughter in November last year after fatally shocking Nowland with a Taser at the Yallambee Lodge aged care facility in May 2023.

White had been called after Nowland, who displayed signs of dementia, was found by staff holding a steak knife in the early hours of the morning.

Body camera footage showed a few minutes of negotiations between White and Nowland before he said “Nah, bugger it” and pulled the trigger on the great-grandmother. She weighed 47.5 kilograms (95 pounds) at the time and could only walk with the aid of a walking frame.

Nowland fell backwards and struck her head on the floor, dying in hospital a week later.

In his sentencing decision on Friday, Judge Harrison said White made a “terrible mistake”.

But Harrison spared White from jail, saying it would be “disproportionate to the objective seriousness of the offence,” as it was not premeditated and occurred in the course of his work as a serving police officer.

data.title: Arrow Minerals' Maiden Resource "Very Encouraging", Says Euroz Hartleys, Shares Up 3%
data.description:
Arrow Minerals' maiden resource was "very encouraging," with a total of 185 million tonnes at 42.3% aluminum oxide and 2.7% silicon dioxide, said Euroz Hartleys in a Thursday note.

The maiden resource includes higher-grade subsets of 138 million tonnes at 44% aluminum oxide and 2.8% silicon dioxide, inclusive of 48 million tonnes at 48.2% aluminum oxide and 2.6% silicon dioxide.

Apart from the company's resources, Euroz Hartleys said it likes its management team with "excellent" development experience. The company also has a "supportive" valuation of AU$28 million market cap.

The research firm also noted that the stock should be considered "very high risk" because the project is still only under an option to acquire.

Euroz Hartleys kept Arrow Minerals' speculative buy rating with an AU$0.08 price target.

The company's shares rose 2% on market close.

data.title: Update: Market Chatter: IAG Chief Says Will Mull Sale of Air Europa Stake
data.description:
(Updates to add IAG's statement)

International Consolidated Airlines Group Chief Executive Officer Luis Gallego told Reuters that the company will consider the potential divestment of its 20% stake in Air Europa.

Reuters on Thursday cited the CEO as saying that the British airline holding company's decision depends on the next move of the Spanish carrier's owner, Globalia, which is currently in talks with Air France-KLM and Deutsche Lufthansa for a possible Air Europa stake deal.

Gallego also said IAG is still interested in acquiring a stake in TAP, but noted that the transaction is contingent on the company's ability to integrate the Portuguese airline into its group.

IAG told MT Newswires it confirms that the Reuters report is accurate.

data.title: Banque Saudi Fransi's Board Proposes Share Buyback
data.description:
Banque Saudi Fransi's (SASE:1050) board recommended a share buyback of 15 million ordinary shares, subject to shareholder approval.

The lender intends to hold the shares as treasury shares for its share-based employee remuneration plans, according to a Thursday filing.

The repurchase will be funded from the bank's own resources.

data.title: Most Romanian stock indices rise, MedLife shines
data.description:
BUCHAREST (Romania), March 28 (SeeNews) - Most Romanian stock indices closed in the green on Thursday, as private healthcare services provider MedLife Group [BSE:M] led blue-chip gainers, Bucharest Stock Exchange data showed.

The total equity turnover fell to 45.5 million lei ($9.85 million/9.14 million euro) on Thursday from 46.3 million lei on Wednesday, according to bourse data.

Banca Transilvania [BSE:TLV] generated the session's largest single turnover of 8.93 million lei, as its share price rose 0.7% to 28.9 lei.

Gas transmission system operator Transgaz [BSE:TGN] saw its shares gain 0.86%, closing at 29.25 lei in the day's second largest trading turnover, of 8.31 million lei.

Shares in MedLife Group closed 3.05% higher at 6.42 lei, pacing blue-chip winners in the third largest trading turnover, of 3.72 million lei. MedLife Group has secured a syndicated loan facility worth 330 million euro, rearranging and expanding its existing credit line by 50 million euro, lead loan coordinator and arranger Banca Comerciala Romana (BCR), said on Wednesday. Last week, MedLife Group said it is entering the market of neighbouring Moldova through acquisition of a majority stake in its peer All Clinic.

Details follow:

BET17,504.64+0.38%
BET-TR38,600.66+0.38%
BET-BK3,261.14-0.22%
BETPlus2,582.45+0.36%
BET-FI60,124.61-2.92%
BET-NG1,262.88+0.45%
BET-XT1,496.29+0.04%
BET-XT-TR3,243.60+0.04%
BET-TRN37,519.72+0.38%
BET-XT-TRN3,159.52+0.04%
BETAeRO859.49+0.86%
BET-EF952.19+0.36%
ROTX39,058.41+0.44%

BET is the first index developed by BVB and represents the reference index for the local capital market. BET reflects the performance of the most traded companies on BVB’s regulated market, excluding financial investment companies (SIFs). It now includes 20 companies.

BET-TR is the first total return index launched by BVB. It is based on the structure of market reference index BET. BET-TR tracks the price changes of its component shares and is adjusted to also reflect the dividends paid by constituent companies.

BET-FI is the first sector index launched by BVB and reflects the price changes of SIFs and of other assimilated entities.

BET-BK was designed to be used as a benchmark by asset managers and other institutional investors.

BET-NG is a sector index which reflects the evolution of all the companies listed on BVB’s regulated market included in the energy and related utilities sector. The maximum index weight a company can hold is 30%.

BET-XT tracks the price changes of the 25 most traded companies listed on BVB’s Regulated Market, including SIFs.

BET-XT-TR is the total return version of BET-XT index, which includes the 25 most traded Romanian companies listed at BVB.

ROTX is an index developed by BVB together with Vienna Stock Exchange. It tracks, in real-time, the price changes of the blue-chip shares traded on the Bucharest Stock Exchange.

BET AeRO is the first index for the AeRO market developed by BVB which reflects the price performance of the representative companies listed on the AeRO market that meet the liquidity and free-float market capitalization criteria. It is a free-float market capitalisation-weighted index, with the maximum weight of 15% for an index constituent.

(1 euro = 4.9772 lei)

data.title: Atlas Elevators' Board Recommends Unchanged Dividend for FY24
data.description:
Atlas Elevators General Trading & Contracting Co.'s (SASE:9578) board proposed an unchanged cash dividend of 0.5 Saudi riyal per share for 2024.

The Saudi Arabian elevator company will disclose the eligibility and payment date for the dividend at a later date, according to a Thursday release.

data.title: Air China's 2024 Attributable Loss Narrows as Revenue Soars; Hong Kong Shares Fall 6%
data.description:
Air China's attributable loss narrowed to 232.6 million yuan in 2024 from 1.04 billion yuan in 2023, according to a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

Loss per share at the airline narrowed to 0.0147 yuan from 0.0674 yuan a year earlier. Analysts at Visible Alpha estimated loss per share at 0.01 yuan.

Revenue grew to 166.7 billion yuan from 141.1 billion yuan in the previous year. Visible Alpha analysts forecast revenue at 169.1 billion yuan.

Shares slid 6% in Hong Kong and 3% in Shanghai during Friday's afternoon trading.

data.title: Singapore Producer Inflation Slows in February
data.description:
The Domestic Supply Price Index in Singapore rose 5.1% year-on-year in February 2025, slowing from over two-year high of 5.5% in the previous month.

Prices eased for food and live animals (4.6% vs 4.8% in January) and beverages and tobacco (0.5% vs 0.6%).

In addition, costs slipped for crude materials (-3.1% vs 0.4%) while deflation worsened for mineral fuels (-4.5% vs -1.3%), animal and vegetables oils, fats and waxes (-4.3% vs -3.6%), chemicals and chemical products (-3.4% vs -2.3%), and manufactured goods (-0.6% vs -0.3%).

Meanwhile, costs increased at a faster pace for both non-oil items (8.9% vs 8.5) and machinery and transport equipment (16% vs 15.2%), while delfation eased for miscellaneous manufactured articles (-1.9% vs -2.9%).

On a monthly basis, the Domestic Supply Price Index fell 0.7%, reversing from a 3.6% gain in January 2025.

data.title: China's Manufacturing Activity Likely Expanded in March — Market Talk
data.description: Activity in China's vast manufacturing sector likely expanded in March despite tariff hits from the U.S., in part thanks to Beijing's stimulus push. The official manufacturing PMI is expected to come in at 50.4 for March, up from February's 50.2, according to a poll of economists by The Wall Street Journal. That would mark the second straight month that the gauge has risen above the 50 mark that separates activity expansion from contraction. China's National Bureau of Statistics is scheduled to release the data Monday. (singaporeeditors@dowjones.com)

data.description:
Woodside Energy Group Ltd :

  • TO DIVEST ITS GREATER ANGOSTURA ASSETS IN TRINIDAD AND TOBAGO FOR $206 MILLION

data.title: Sensex Slightly Drops But Points to Monthly Gains
data.description:
India's equities slipped 85 points, or 0.1%, to 77,521 in morning deals on the final trading session of the month, snapping gains from the prior session and weighed down by the tech, pharmaceuticals, and auto sectors.

The BSE Sensex tracked a fall on Wall Street overnight amid concerns over a potential trade war following President Trump's announcement of new tariffs on foreign-made autos.

Traders continued to monitor the US-India trade talks, focusing on key sectors, including pharmaceuticals and autos, ahead of the Eid al-Fitr holidays on Monday.

Capping the fall was a Reuters poll showing that the RBI is expected to cut interest rates for the second consecutive meeting due to easing inflation.

Among the early losers were Mahindra & Mahindra (-3.3%), Infosys (-1.7%), and Sun Pharmaceuticals (-1.2%).

For the month, the index is set to surge around 5.9%, recovering from a fall in the previous month, buoyed by renewed foreign inflows amid optimism about domestic economic data.

data.title: Orora Confirms French Competition Authority' Review on Alleged Anticompetitive Practices
data.description:
Orora confirmed that the French Competition Authority is investigating potential anticompetitive practices in the French beverage glass container industry, involving activities before the company's acquisition of Saverglass, according to a Friday filing by the Australian bourse.

The company is cooperating with the investigation, the filing said.

Shares of the company fell 9% on market close and earlier reached an all-time low.

data.title: Deutsche Bank Names Successor to Outgoing CFO, Extends CEO's Contract
data.description:
Deutsche Bank Chief Financial Officer James von Moltke plans to step down at the end of his term in June 2026.

The German bank said Thursday that Morgan Stanley's deputy CFO Raja Akram will join as designated CFO on Oct. 1, 2025, its management board on Jan. 1, 2026, and become CFO after a transition period.

The bank's supervisory board also approved a three-year extension for Chief Executive Officer Christian Sewing's mandate until April 2029.

data.title: Australia's Woodside Energy to divest Greater Angostura assets for $206 million
data.description: Australia's Woodside Energy said on Friday it had struck a deal to divest its Greater Angostura assets in Trinidad and Tobago to London-based oil and gas firm Perenco for $206 million.

data.title: Sembcorp Industries Signs 25-Year Renewable Energy Purchase Agreement
data.description:
Sembcorp Industries through its wholly owned subsidiary, Sembcorp Solar Singapore, entered into a 25-year renewable energy purchase agreement with Malkoha, a subsidiary of Meta Platforms, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Friday.

Under the agreement, Sembcorp will build, own and operate a floating solar photovoltaic system of around 150MWp at the Kranji Reservoir.

Construction of the floating solar farm is expected to start in the first half of 2027.

data.title: Tanvex BioPharma Elects Chairman
data.description:
Tanvex BioPharma elected Sheng Pao-Shi as its chairman on Thursday, according to a same-day Taiwan Exchange filing.

Sheng will succeed Chen Lin-Cheng in the role following the expiration of his term. Sheng also serves as chairman of Bora Pharmaceuticals, the biotech company said.

data.title: Advanced Medical Solutions Yet to Receive Offer from Montagu Private Equity
data.description:
Advanced Medical Solutions Group said Thursday it is yet to receive a proposal from Montagu Private Equity.

The British wound care products manufacturer made the statement after the private equity investor confirmed media rumors that it is exploring the possibility of taking over the group.

Advanced Medical cautioned that there is no guarantee a formal offer will be made. Montagu has until April 24 to clarify whether or not it will make a formal offer.

data.title: Al-Rajhi Co. for Cooperative Insurance to Provide Services Under One-year Deal
data.description:
Al-Rajhi Co. for Cooperative Insurance (SASE:8230) signed a one-year deal with Al Rajhi Bank (SASE:1120) to provide insurance coverage services to real estate finance portfolio customers.

The Saudi Arabian multi-line insurance company said Thursday the deal is expected to be valued at over 5% of its total revenue based on its latest financial statements.

data.description:

data.title: Saudi Arabia's iOud Posts Lower FY24 Net Profit; Revenue Up
data.description:
Intelligent Oud Co. for Trading (SASE:9579), d/b/a iOud, said Thursday that its net profit for full-year 2024 fell, while revenue rose year over year.

Net profit for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 4.7 million Saudi riyals, compared with 5.3 million riyals earlier. EPS moved to 2.84 riyals from 3.09 riyals.

The Saudi Arabia-listed cosmetic products supplier's revenue was 26.7 million riyals, compared with 25.2 million riyals previously.

data.title: China’s 10-Year Bond Yield Drops on High Debt Issuance
data.description:
China’s 10-year government bond yield dropped to around 1.87% on Friday, as the government increased debt issuance aimed at stimulating growth and stabilizing the bond market.

In the first quarter of 2025, the Ministry of Finance raised a record CNY 1.45 trillion through sovereign bonds, three times more than the same period last year and marking a record for any first quarter.

This unprecedented surge in debt issuance highlights Beijing’s growing urgency to ramp up fiscal spending in response to multiple economic pressures, while also seeking to alleviate concerns over last year’s bond rally, which amplified risks to financial stability.

Meanwhile, external pressures have intensified, as US President Donald Trump’s new tariffs on Chinese goods have added to the economic uncertainty.

In response, Chinese policymakers have unveiled plans to issue CNY 11.86 trillion in new government bonds this year, along with raising the budget deficit target to an unprecedented 4% of GDP.

data.title: Som Datt Finance to Raise Up to INR500 Million via Rights Issue
data.description:
Som Datt Finance said its board approved a rights issue of equity shares to raise up to 500 million Indian rupees, according to an Indian bourse filing on Thursday.

The issue is subject to regulatory approvals, the filing said.

data.title: South Carolina dismisses its staking lawsuit against Coinbase, joining Vermont
data.description:
South Carolina has become the latest US state to dismiss its lawsuit against crypto exchange Coinbase over its staking services, which had accused the crypto exchange of offering unregistered securities.

The lawsuit was officially dismissed in a joint stipulation between the crypto exchange and the South Carolina Attorney General’s securities division on March 27.

“South Carolina just joined Vermont to dismiss its unfounded staking lawsuit against Coinbase,” the firm’s chief legal officer, Paul Grewal, said in a March 27 X post.

“This is not just a victory for us, but for American consumers and we hope it's a sign of things to come in the few states left that restrict staking.”

South Carolina and Vermont were two of 10 US states that took legal action against Coinbase's staking services on June 6, 2023 — the same day that the federal securities regulator filed its lawsuit against the crypto exchange.

The Securities and Exchange Commission officially dismissed that lawsuit on Feb. 27, 2025.

The other eight US states that filed enforcement action similar to South Carolina were Alabama, California, Illinois, Kentucky, Maryland, New Jersey, Washington and Wisconsin. 

Grewal said he hoped to see other states follow suit, and that South Carolina residents lost an estimated $2 million in staking rewards as a result of the lawsuit.

“The 52 million Americans who own crypto deserve commonsense consumer protections and clear rules,” he said. “We applaud South Carolina for standing up for justice and hope the remaining states with bans on staking will take notice.”

South Carolina introduces Bitcoin reserve bill

Meanwhile, a state lawmaker has just introduced the “Strategic Digital Assets Reserve Act of South Carolina” on March 27, which could see the state treasurer allocate up to 10% of certain state funds to cryptocurrencies such as Bitcoin (BTC).

Unlike most US state crypto reserve bills, North Carolina’s House Bill 4256, introduced by Rep. Jordan Pace, mentioned Bitcoin on several occasions for the Strategic Digital Assets Reserve that the bill seeks to establish.

The bill allows South Carolina’s treasurer, currently Curtis Loftis, to establish a Bitcoin reserve that exceeds no more than 1 million Bitcoin — a high ceiling that the US federal government is also looking to reach or exceed with its recently established Strategic Bitcoin Reserve.

The treasurer would be able to add Bitcoin to South Carolina’s General Fund, the Budget Stabilization Reserve Fund any other investment fund that they manage.

While no mention of stablecoins, non-fungible tokens, Ether (ETH) or any other crypto tokens was made, the House bill said the Strategic Digital Assets Reserve wouldn’t be limited to Bitcoin.

According to Bitcoin Law, 42 Bitcoin reserve bills have been introduced at the state level in 19 states, and 36 of those 42 bills remain live.

Earlier this month, US President Donald Trump signed an executive order to create a Strategic Bitcoin Reserve and a Digital Asset Stockpile, both of which will initially use cryptocurrency forfeited in government criminal cases.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Friday at 1 AM ET
data.description:
Major U.S. Stock Indexes Close Lower After Tariffs Shake Automakers

Stock indexes fell for a second day after the U.S. unveiled new tariffs on automakers.

BOJ Policy Board More Cautious of U.S. Policy Risks

The Bank of Japan's policy board has become more cautious about uncertainties regarding U.S. economic policies, according to the summary of opinions of its March meeting.

Inflation Might Be Stuck for Now. The Fed's Preferred Metric Comes Friday.

Americans finally got some good news in the form of cooling inflation earlier this month. But the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge is set to reverse that trend.

Fed's Collins supports holding interest rates steady for 'longer time'

The U.S. economic outlook is cloudy and the Federal Reserve should be patient about adjusting interest rates, Boston Fed President Susan Collins said Thursday.

Canada Aims to Respond to Latest U.S. Tariffs When Trump's Next Salvo Is Fired

Prime Minister Mark Carney pledged to respond to Trump's latest tariffs targeting the automotive industry with strong counter measures, but held off introducing further tariffs until it becomes clear what the president will introduce next week.

Bank of Mexico Makes Second Straight Half-Point Rate Cut

The Bank of Mexico lowered its benchmark interest rate by half of a percentage point for the second meeting in a row as inflation eased and the economy continued to slow, and said further cuts of that size are possible.

U.S. Fourth-Quarter GDP Revised Higher

The pace of economic growth picked up slightly in the final months of 2024, government data showed, but most economists expect a slowdown in the first quarter.

White House Makes Fresh Economic Demands of Ukraine as Russia Talks Stall

President Trump is putting pressure on Ukraine for an economic deal with greater concessions from Kyiv, just as Washington's efforts to get Russia to agree to a cease-fire have stalled.

Trump's Car Tariffs Are Worse Than the Worst-Case Scenario. GM Stock Tumbles.

President Trump announces 25% tariffs on cars imported to the U.S. from anywhere in the world. The policy is much worse than Wall Street expected.

The U.S. trade deficit remains near a record high - and will be a drag on economic growth

The deficit in February remained much larger than the $135.5 billion trade gap forecast by Wall Street economists polled by Econoday.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Friday at 1 AM ET
data.description:
CoreWeave Prices IPO at $40, Well Below Earlier Estimates

The AI start-up priced its stock at $40, well below recent expectations for $47 to $55 a share. The listing has been seen as a test for both AI and the market for new stock.

Lululemon Outlook Misses as Consumer Confidence Falls

The activewear company said it expects sales of $11.15 billion to $11.3 billion for its fiscal 2025, the high-end of which is in line with analyst views.

Trump Warned U.S. Automakers Not to Raise Prices in Response to Tariffs

The threat came in a call earlier this month, leaving CEOs worried they would face punishment if prices increased.

Signal Ascends From Hacker Passion Project to Washington's Top Messaging App

Downloads of the app surged in the past week after the Atlantic published White House officials' texts about a military strike.

Trump's Car Tariffs Are Worse Than the Worst-Case Scenario. GM Stock Tumbles.

President Trump announces 25% tariffs on cars imported to the U.S. from anywhere in the world. The policy is much worse than Wall Street expected.

Ubisoft Gets $1.25 Billion Investment From Tencent for New Gaming Unit

Ubisoft Entertainment said it is forming a new unit that would house its Assassin's Creed, Far Cry, and Tom Clancy's Rainbow Six games, with Tencent Holdings investing 1.16 billion euros ($1.25 billion) for a minority stake in the subsidiary.

Ferrari to Raise Prices on Some Models in Response to U.S. Tariffs

Ferrari said it will shield customers from the brunt of U.S. tariffs by absorbing the cost of higher import duties on certain models, while passing on only some of the impact through higher pricing on others.

Freight Startup Flexport Misses Profitability Target

Founder and CEO of digital forwarder, Ryan Petersen, says company will hit goal in 2025, paving way for potential public offering.

Repsol to Combine U.K. North Sea Upstream Business With NEO Energy

The joint venture could deliver synergies exceeding $1 billion.

H&M Earnings Miss Forecasts on Higher Discounts and Costs

Sales rose 2% but momentum slowed in the final month of the quarter.

data.title: EMEA Morning Briefing: Markets in Wait-And-See Mode Ahead of Reciprocal Tariffs
data.description:
MARKET WRAPS

Watch For:

EU business and consumer surveys; U.K. GDP, trade, retail sales, balance of payments, business investment; Germany Gfk consumer climate survey, unemployment; France CPI, consumer spending; no major corporate trading updates expected

Opening Call:

European stock futures were edging lower in tandem with broad declines in Asian equities. The dollar gained slightly; Treasury yields were mostly lower; Oil futures declined, and gold gained.

Equities:

European futures pointed slightly lower as investors and analysts struggle to digest the impact of the Trump administration's latest tariffs.

The U.S. stock market fell for the second day in a row on Thursday after trading sideways for most of the session.

After sharp losses earlier in the month, bleeding related to tariff news appears to have slowed. Markets are in a bit of a holding pattern as they await the Trump administration's plans for reciprocal tariffs starting on April 2. Some Wall Street analysts are taking solace in a run of decent economic data reports, and it isn't clear how determined Trump is to stick with the tariffs long-term.

Fed officials on Thursday indicated that the central bank was unlikely to change interest rates until the tariff fog starts to clear. On tap today is the Fed's preferred inflation gauge- the personal consumption expenditures price index for February. Economists forecast a 2.5% year-over-year increase, matching the January figure.

Forex:

Sterling could rise modestly against the euro this year as the U.K. economy should outperform the eurozone, Bank of America analysts said. President Trump's tariff plans are targeting countries with large surpluses. The U.K. falls out of this category while it also benefits from a largely service-focused economy, they said.

Europe's fiscal spending pledges are supportive of the euro through flows into European equities. However, this is likely via a rotation from U.S. equities rather than U.K. equities. The European Central Bank could cut interest rates more than markets expect while the Bank of England remains cautious, they said.

BofA expects the euro to fall to 0.8000 pounds by the fourth quarter.

Bonds:

The gilt market's volatility risks resurfacing due to the vulnerable state of U.K. public finances and heavy gilt supply ahead, HSBC U.K. rates strategist Daniela Russell said. Concerns about the impact of tariffs on the U.K. economy could also weigh on investor sentiment, she said.

"Attention can now shift back towards the macroeconomic data and global developments, including around possible reciprocal U.S. tariffs, and how they will affect the Bank of England outlook," she said.

Energy:

Oil prices were lower early Friday. The oil market could see growing volatility and pullback risks, said Maria Agustina Patti, a financial markets strategist consultant at Exness. Prices could be supported by tighter global supply concerns, driven by U.S. tariff threats on Venezuelan oil buyers and ongoing sanctions on Iranian oil, Patti said.

Traders may stay cautious about any developments that may exacerbate supply disruptions, Patti added.

Metals:

Gold was slightly higher after hitting an all-time high overnight. "A series of economic and geopolitical factors have fueled this surge," XS.com's Antonio Di Giacomo said. "Among them, the new tariff measures announced by U.S. President Trump stand out, as they have created uncertainty in international markets," the financial markets analyst said.

This protectionist stance has boosted trade tensions and spurred investors to seek refuge in safe-haven assets like gold, the analyst added.

--

Copper prices fell amid profit-taking. Expectations that the Trump administration will announce a copper-specific levy in the coming weeks has fueled a recent rise in prices, ANZ Research analysts said.

However, any pullback in copper prices is likely to be relatively short-lived, with strong fundamentals providing a backstop. China's copper imports are strong while inventories are falling, it noted.

--

Iron ore prices were lower, weighed by subdued demand. Analysts remain cautious that the current demand may not sustain given regulatory policies, Baocheng Futures said. However, iron ore's strong fundamentals could support prices ahead, it added.

TODAY'S TOP HEADLINES

Inflation Might Be Stuck for Now. The Fed's Preferred Metric Comes Friday.

Americans finally received some good news in the form of cooling inflation earlier this month. But the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge is set to reverse that trend with the latest data release on Friday.

Earlier in the month, the latest data from the Consumer Price Index and Producer Price Index provided Americans some relief that overall consumer and wholesale pricing pressures eased in February. But under the hood, factors from both indexes that the Bureau of Economic Analysis uses to calculate the Personal Consumption Expenditures Price Index point to a firmer February print for the Fed's preferred measure.

Fed's Collins supports holding interest rates steady for 'longer time'

The U.S. economic outlook is cloudy and the Federal Reserve should be patient about adjusting interest rates, Boston Fed President Susan Collins said Thursday.

"I strongly believed at the meeting that we had last week that holding our rate steady was an appropriate policy stance and, looking forward, I would expect holding it steady for a longer time is likely to be appropriate," Collins said in a moderated discussion sponsored by the Insurance Women's Investment Network, 100 Women in Finance and Wellington Management

Ferrari to Raise Prices on Some Models in Response to U.S. Tariffs

Ferrari plans price increases of up to 10% on some of its models after the introduction of U.S. auto tariffs, as the sports-car maker races to mitigate a hit from the duties.

U.S. President Trump said he will impose 25% tariffs on all vehicles imported to the country from April 2, a move that will hit Ferrari more than most European manufacturers as it only produces its cars in Italy.

Ubisoft Gets $1.25 Billion Investment From Tencent for New Gaming Unit

Ubisoft Entertainment said it is forming a new unit that would house its Assassin's Creed, Far Cry, and Tom Clancy's Rainbow Six games, with Tencent Holdings investing 1.16 billion euros ($1.25 billion) for a minority stake in the subsidiary.

The French videogame company said Thursday that the deal with Tencent values the newly formed subsidiary at an enterprise value-including debt-of EUR4 billion.

CoreWeave Prices IPO at $40, Well Below Estimates. It's Another Headwind for AI and Public Markets.

CoreWeave, a rapidly growing AI cloud company, priced its initial public offering at $40 a share on Thursday night, well below an expected range of $47 to $55.

At the $40 IPO price, CoreWeave has a market value of $23 billion. The company also sold 23% fewer shares than it originally intended.

Trump Warned U.S. Automakers Not to Raise Prices in Response to Tariffs

When President Trump convened CEOs of some of the country's top automakers for a call earlier this month, he issued a warning: They better not raise car prices because of tariffs.

Trump told the executives that the White House would look unfavorably on such a move, leaving some of them rattled and worried they would face punishment if they increased prices, people with knowledge of the call said.

Write to singaporeeditors@dowjones.com

Expected Major Events for Friday

data.title: SingTel's Tech Services Arm, Globe Telecom to Form Joint Venture
data.description:
SingTel's technology services arm, NCS, entered into a joint venture with Philippine-based Globe Telecom , according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Thursday.

Under the deal, NCS agreed to acquire a majority stake in Yondu, the IT arm of Globe Telecom.

The joint venture, to be renamed NCS Philippines, is expected to increase its workforce in the Philippines to more than 1,200 professionals and accelerate the expansion of NCS' global delivery network.
